title: Multiply with 5 and 10
essential_question: What strategies can you use to multiply with 5 and 10?

pretest.question: Mrs. Hinely has 10 rose bushes with 6 roses each. How many roses?

learn.title: Patterns for ×5
learn.content: When you multiply by 5, the product always ends in 0 or 5. Skip count: 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35, 40, 45, 50. Think of counting nickels!

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.2 'Multiply with 5 and 10' pp.143-146
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic D & Module 3 — Multiplying with Units of 5 and 10
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Patterns for ×10
learn.content: When you multiply by 10, just put a 0 at the end of the other number. 10 × 7 = 70. 10 × 3 = 30. Think of counting dimes!

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.2 'Multiply with 5 and 10' pp.143-146
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic D & Module 3 — Multiplying with Units of 5 and 10
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: ×5 Is Half of ×10
learn.content: Multiplying by 5 is half of multiplying by 10. If 10 × 6 = 60, then 5 × 6 = 30 (half of 60). This is a fast way to find ×5 facts when you already know ×10.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.2 'Multiply with 5 and 10' pp.143-146
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic D & Module 3 — Multiplying with Units of 5 and 10
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Arrays for ×5 and ×10
learn.content: An array of 5 rows by 4 dots shows 5 × 4 = 20. An array of 10 rows by 4 dots shows 10 × 4 = 40. Notice: doubling the number of rows from 5 to 10 doubles the product.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.2 'Multiply with 5 and 10' pp.143-146
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic D & Module 3 — Multiplying with Units of 5 and 10
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Money Helps You See It
learn.content: A nickel is 5 cents and a dime is 10 cents. 6 nickels = 5 × 6 = 30 cents. 6 dimes = 10 × 6 = 60 cents. 6 dimes is exactly twice 6 nickels — just like ×10 is twice ×5.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.2 'Multiply with 5 and 10' pp.143-146
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic D & Module 3 — Multiplying with Units of 5 and 10
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: ×5 끝자리 규칙 — 0 또는 5
learn.content: ×5 곱은 항상 0 또는 5로 끝납니다. 짝수 × 5 → 0으로 끝남 (예: 6 × 5 = 30, 8 × 5 = 40). 홀수 × 5 → 5로 끝남 (예: 7 × 5 = 35, 9 × 5 = 45). 또 다른 빠른 방법: 다른 인자의 절반을 십의 자리, 그리고 0(짝수) 또는 5(홀수)를 일의 자리에. 흔한 실수: 끝자리는 맞추지만 십의 자리를 잘못 넣음 (예: 6×5=35로 답).

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.2 'Multiply with 5 and 10' pp.143-146
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic D & Module 3 — Multiplying with Units of 5 and 10
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: ×10은 단순히 0 붙이기
learn.content: ×10의 가장 빠른 방법: 다른 인자 뒤에 0을 한 개 붙이기. 예: 10 × 7 = 70, 10 × 9 = 90. 이 규칙은 1자리 수에 한해 항상 작동 (10진법의 마법). 거꾸로도: 10 × ? = 90 → 90에서 0을 떼면 9가 미지수. 주의: 모든 0이 끝나는 수가 ×10 곱은 아님 (예: 80은 ×10이 맞지만 70도, 90도 모두 ×10임).

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.2 'Multiply with 5 and 10' pp.143-146
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic D & Module 3 — Multiplying with Units of 5 and 10
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: ×5·×10 즉답 전략 요약
learn.content: 전략 1 ×10 = 0 붙이기: 10 × n = n0. 전략 2 ×5 = ×10의 절반: 5 × n = (10 × n) ÷ 2.   예: 5 × 8 → 10 × 8 = 80 → 80 ÷ 2 = 40. 전략 3 끝자리 점검: 짝수×5는 0, 홀수×5는 5로 끝. 전략 4 동전 모델: nickel=5¢, dime=10¢.   6 nickels = 30¢, 6 dimes = 60¢ — dime은 nickel의 두 배.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.2 'Multiply with 5 and 10' pp.143-146
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic D & Module 3 — Multiplying with Units of 5 and 10
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

hints: Multiplying by 10 adds a 0 to the other number.

feedback.incorrect: 10 × 8 = 80. Just write the 8, then a 0. Answer is C.

try.question: 5 × ? = 35. What is the missing factor?
hints: Skip count by 5s and stop at 35. Count how many jumps.

feedback.incorrect: Count by 5s to 35: you land on 35 at the seventh count. Answer is C.

hints: Each day adds 10. Multiply 10 by the number of days.

feedback.incorrect: 10 cents a day for 8 days = 10 × 8 = 80 cents. Answer is C.

practice_r2.question: Which number is NOT a product of 5?
practice_r2.explanation: 22 doesn't end in 0 or 5. All ×5 products do.
hints: Products of 5 always end in 0 or 5.
Check the last digit of each.

feedback.incorrect: ×5 products end in 0 or 5. Only 22 doesn't. Answer is B.

practice_r2.question: Which number IS a product of 10?
practice_r2.explanation: 60 ends in 0. Products of 10 always end in 0.
hints: Products of 10 always end in 0 (exactly one 0 for these).

feedback.incorrect: Products of 10 end in 0. Only 60 qualifies. Answer is C.

feedback.incorrect: 10 × 8 = 80, half is 40, and 5 × 8 = 40. They match. True.
